perturbation.original_text: Section 12.07. Waiver of Jury Trial. THE PARTIES HERETO HEREBY IRREVOCABLY WAIVE ------ -- ---- ----- ANY AND ALL RIGHT TO TRIAL BY JURY IN ANY LEGAL PROCEEDING ARISING OUT OF OR RELATING TO THIS AGREEMENT OR THE TRANSACTIONS CONTEMPLATED HEREBY OR THEREBY.
perturbation.changed_text: Section 8.04. Waiver of Jury Trial. THE PARTIES HERETO HEREBY IRREVOCABLY WAIVE ------ -- ---- ----- ANY AND ALL RIGHT TO TRIAL BY JURY IN ANY LEGAL PROCEEDING ARISING OUT OF OR RELATING TO THIS AGREEMENT OR THE TRANSACTIONS CONTEMPLATED HEREBY OR THEREBY.
perturbation.explanation: The jury trial waiver has been moved from the 'Miscellaneous' section to a new, out-of-place 'Information Exchanges' subsection, making it less prominent and potentially overlooked. Jury waivers must be conspicuous and knowingly made.
perturbation.law_explanation: Moving the jury trial waiver to an unexpected location within the contract undermines its conspicuousness and could lead a court to find that the waiver was not knowingly and voluntarily made, violating the implied requirements for waiver of constitutional rights.
perturbation.scraped_snippet_2: Seventh Amendment | U.S. Constitution | US Law | LII / Legal Information Institute Please help us improve our site! × No thank you Seventh Amendment The Seventh Amendment continues a practice from English common law of distinguishing civil claims which must be tried before a jury (absent waiver by the parties) from claims and issues that may be heard by a judge alone. It only governs federal civil courts and has no application to civil courts set up by the states when those courts are hearing only disputes of state law . Learn more... Primary tabs Amendment VII In suits at common law, where the value in controversy shall exceed twenty dollars, the right of trial by jury shall be preserved, and no fact tried by a jury, shall be otherwise reexamined in any court of the United States, than according to the rules of the common law. Wex Resources Criminal Law Criminal Procedure Jury Other Resources US Code: Title 18 Federal Rules of Criminal Procedure ‹ Sixth Amendment up Eighth Amendment › U.S. Constitution Toolbox Explanation of the Constitution - from the Congressional Research Service
perturbation.justification: The structural flaw introduced by moving the jury trial waiver to an unexpected location creates a legal contradiction. The Perturbation Explanation states that this move makes the waiver 'less prominent and potentially overlooked,' and the Law Explanation asserts that it 'undermines its conspicuousness' potentially leading a court to find the waiver was not knowingly made. While Scraped Law Snippet 1 is missing, Scraped Law Snippet 2, referencing the Seventh Amendment, states that the right to trial by jury shall be preserved (absent waiver by the parties). By making the waiver less conspicuous through its placement, the changed text weakens the likelihood that the waiver was knowingly and voluntarily made, potentially violating the requirement that such waivers be clear and conspicuous, hence contradicting the preservation of the right to a jury trial unless explicitly waived.
